title: Drop the duplicates
prompt: Write dedupe(lst) that returns a sorted list of the unique values in lst. dedupe([3,1,2,3,1]) is [1,2,3].

help.plain: Remove repeats, then return the values in sorted order.
help.analogy: Like collapsing a guest list where people signed in twice — one entry each.
help.mnemonic: set() drops duplicates; sorted() puts them in order.
help.nudge: What built-in collection automatically throws away duplicates?

details.restate: Return the distinct values of a list, sorted ascending. Repeats collapse to a single entry.

terms.meaning: a collection with no duplicates and no order
